note:
Robin.io承诺，它将为集装箱化的应用程序带来云原生数据管理功能，并支持备份和恢复、快照、回滚等标准操作。它可以做到所有这些，同时提供裸机性能和对所有主要云的支持。该服务本质上与实际使用的数据库无关，并支持PostgreSQL、MySQL、MongoDB、Redis、MariaDB、Cassandra、Elasticsearch等。
罗宾创始人兼首席执行官帕塔·西塔拉(Partha Seetala)说：“罗宾云本地存储可以在任何基于库伯内斯的平台和任何云上处理任何工作负载。”凭借存储、拍摄快照、备份、克隆、迁移和保护数据的功能-所有这些功能都只需使用最简单的命令-Robin Cloud Native Storage为开发人员和DevOps团队提供了一个超级简单但高性能的工具，用于在Kubernetes上快速部署和管理他们的企业工作负载。“。
新的免费版本允许团队管理多达5个节点和5TB的存储。这里的承诺是，这是一个终身免费的服务，该公司显然希望它能让企业感受到这项服务，然后随着时间的推移升级到付费企业计划。
在谈到这些企业计划时，该公司今天还宣布，它将转向基于消费的定价计划，起步价为每节点小时0.42美元(尽管它也提供年度订阅)。企业计划包括全天候支持，不限制节点数量或存储容量。
Robin核心存储服务的新特性包括对Helm Charts(其中Helm是Kubernetes包管理器)的数据管理支持、指定数据应该确切驻留的位置(主要是为了使其接近计算资源)的能力，以及确保依赖于分布式数据库和数据平台的有状态应用程序的可用性的关联策略。
note_en:
Robin  .io promises that it brings cloud-native data management capabilities to containerized applications with support for standard operations like backup and recovery, snapshots, rollbacks and more. It does all of that while offering bare-metal performance and support for all major clouds. The service is essentially agnostic to the actual database being used and offers support for the likes of PostgreSQL,  MySQL,   MongoDB, Redis, MariaDB, Cassandra, Elasticsearch and others.
   “Robin Cloud Native Storage works with any workload on any Kubernetes-based platform and on any cloud,” said Robin founder and CEO Partha Seetala. “With capabilities for storing, taking snapshots, backing up, cloning, migrating and securing data — all with the simplest of commands —  Robin Cloud Native Storage offers developers and DevOps teams a super simple yet highly performant tool for quickly deploying and managing their enterprise workloads on Kubernetes.”
 The new free version lets teams manage up to 5 nodes and 5TB of storage. The promise here is that this a free-for-life offering and the company obviously expects that it allows enterprises to get a feel for the service and then upgrade to its paid enterprise plans over time.
 Talking about those enterprise plans, the company also today announced that it is moving to a consumption-based pricing plan, starting at $0.42 per node-hour (though it also offers annual subscriptions). The enterprise plan includes 24×7 support and doesn’t limit the number of nodes or storage capacity.
 Among the new features to Robin’s core storage service are data management support for Helm Charts (where  Helm is the Kubernetes package manager), the ability to specify where exactly the data should reside (which is mostly meant to keep it close to the compute resources) and affinity policies that ensure availability for stateful applications that rely on distributed databases and data platforms.
title: Robin.io发布其云原生Kubernetes存储解决方案的免费版本
title_en: Robin.io launches a free version of its cloud-native Kubernetes storage solution

note:
这家初创公司成立于2015年，其使命是通过协作(以及少量人工智能)简化发票管理。有趣的是，斯坦普利表示，它当时对在协作套件之外提供支付产品不感兴趣，而是专注于采购支付的过程。
这一最新声明标志着该公司思路的转变。联合创始人兼首席执行官埃亚尔·费尔德曼(EYAL Feldman)解释说，与客户的对话显示，许多组织对目前的B2B支付格局感到失望。
组织有几种选择：剪切并邮寄自己的纸质支票，使用ACH，或者与支付提供商签约使用“电子支付”。
剪切和邮寄支票是一项史前的、耗时的活动，并不真正属于2020年，而ACH(成本非常低、固定成本)通常将多笔交易分组为一笔金额，这使得会计很难对单个行项目的购买进行对账。
“在‘电子支付’的误导性旗帜下，[支付提供商]向AP部门提供回扣，并承诺供应商更快地付款，”费尔德曼在一篇博客文章中解释道。然而，为了让卖家获得付款，他们必须接受虚拟信用卡支付，每笔交易最高可收取3.5%的信用卡手续费。
许多支付提供商不会将从发票和交易中提取的数据提供给组织，以此作为保持粘性的一种方式。
斯坦普利的客户为这家过去与支付无关的初创公司阐明了这些问题。随着Stampli Direct Pay的推出，该公司的支付仍然很灵活，允许组织与其现有的或不同的支付提供商合作。但斯坦普利现在提供了一个旨在解决其中许多行业问题的选择。
因为Stampli的核心产品已经跟踪了每笔交易的所有上下文和相关信息，所以这些信息在支付审批过程中很容易获得。Direct Pay还提供ACH作为支付选项，但将单独的交易分开以便于对账。对于那些想要坚持使用支票的客户，Stampli Direct Pay提供了一项服务，允许客户批准直接来自他们的银行账户并带有签名的数字支票，Stampli负责打印、盖章和邮寄。
Stampli还提供了一个供应商支付门户网站，该门户网站提取每个供应商所需的数据，并让客户拥有这些数据，这些数据可以下载并带给另一个支付提供商。
通常，团队购买产品或服务，这些发票最终进入财务部门，几乎没有上下文，在组织内部引发了一场鸭子鹅游戏，因为会计师试图获得他们向供应商付款所需的信息和批准。
到目前为止，斯坦普利已经筹集了3200万美元，它建立了一个协作平台，允许非会计师以一种简单明了的方式参与发票管理流程。每一张发票都成了沟通的枢纽，让不同部门的人都可以填空。回答有关购买的问题。Stampli还使用机器学习来识别与成本分配、管理审批工作流以及需要从发票中提取的数据相关的模式。
每一张发票都变成了自己的通信枢纽，允许跨部门的人填写空白处并回答问题，以便尽可能高效地处理付款。此外，Stampli使用机器学习来识别组织如何分配成本、管理审批工作流程以及从发票中提取哪些数据的模式。
随着Direct Pay的推出，斯坦普利准备与各种具有明显差异化优势的新竞争对手展开竞争。该公司每年处理的发票超过130亿美元。
团队也已经发展到100多名员工。根据费尔德曼的数据，该公司56%的美国劳动力是非白人，33%的高管领导团队是女性。
note_en:
The startup launched back in 2015 with a mission to simplify invoice management through collaboration (and a dash of AI). Interestingly, Stampli said it was uninterested at the time in providing a payments product alongside its collaborative suite, focusing instead on the process of procure to pay.
 This latest announcement marks a shift in the company’s thinking. Cofounder and CEO  Eyal Feldman explained that conversations with customers revealed just how frustrated many organizations are with the current B2B payments landscape.
  Organizations have several options: cut and mail their own paper checks, use ACH, or sign on with a payments provider to use ‘e-payments.’
 Cutting and mailing checks is a pre-historic, time-intensive activity that doesn’t really belong in 2020, while ACH (which comes at a very low, flat cost) often groups multiple transactions into a single sum, making it difficult for accounting to reconcile individual line item purchases.
 “Under the misleading banner of “e-payments,” [payments providers] offer AP departments a rebate and promise vendors faster payment,” explained Feldman in a blog post. “However, in order for vendors to get the payment, they must accept payments as virtual credit cards, which come with up to a 3.5% credit card fee per transaction.”
 And many payments providers do not provide the data extracted from invoices and transactions back to the organization as a way to stay sticky.
 Stampli’s customers illuminated these problems for the startup, which used to be payments agnostic. With the launch of Stampli Direct Pay, the company is still payments flexible, letting organizations work with their existing or different payments providers. But Stampli now offers an option that aims to resolve many of these industry issues.
 Because Stampli’s core product already tracks all the contextual and relevant info for every transaction, that information is readily available during payment approval. Direct Pay also offers ACH as a payment option, but separates individual transactions out for easy reconciliation. And for customers who want to stick with checks, Stampli Direct Pay offers a service that allows customers to approve digital checks which come directly from their bank account with their signature, with Stampli handling printing, stamping, and mailing.
 Stampli also offers a vendor payment portal that extracts the needed data for each vendor and lets the customer own that data, which can be downloaded and taken to another payment provider.
  Usually, teams purchase products or services and those invoices end up in the finance department with little to no context, setting off a game of duck duck goose within the organization as accountants try to get the information and approvals they need to pay out that vendor.
 Stampli, which has raised  $32 million to date, built out a collaborative platform that allows non-accountants to participate in the invoice management process in a way that’s straightforward and simple. Each invoice becomes a communications hub, allowing folks across various departments fill in the blanks and. answer questions about the purchase. Stampli also uses machine learning to recognize patterns around allocating costs, managing approval workflows, and the data that needs to be extracted from invoices.
 Each invoice is turned into its own communications hub, allowing people across departments to fill in the blanks and answer questions so that payments are handled as efficiently as possible. Moreover, Stampli uses machine learning to recognize patterns around how the organization allocates cost, manages approval workflows and what data is extracted from invoices.
 With the launch of Direct Pay, Stampli is poised to take on a variety of new competitors with an obvious differentiator. The company has processed more than $13 billion in invoices annually.
 The team has also grown to more than 100 employees. Fifty-six percent of the company’s US workforce is non-white and 33 percent of the executive leadership team is female, according to Feldman.
title: Stampli是一家发票管理软件初创公司，它推出了一款支付产品
title_en: Stampli, an invoice management software startup, launches a payments product

note:
如今，在大卫·萨克斯(David Sacks)、Slake、Gradient Ventures、Y Combinator和其他个人投资者的260万美元种子投资的帮助下，他们可以继续实现这个梦想。
吸引这项投资的是几位年轻的创始人，他们热衷于让构建数据仪表盘变得简单，而不需要工程师或数据分析师等专家的帮助。
“从本质上说，我们所做的就是帮助公司用尽可能少的代码或技术知识建立他们的业务指标仪表盘。这样做的副产品是，公司中的任何人都可以为他们的团队建立自己的指标，“联合创始人Xavier Pladevall告诉TechCrunch。
最终用户可以连接到越来越多的数据源，Index处理构建查询并为用户显示数据，而无需数据科学家或数据分析师的帮助。目前，这包括用于CRM数据的Salesforce和Hubpot，条带支付数据，以及Postgres和MongoDB的某些数据库。
当创始人构建产品时，他们希望只有两名创始人，也许还有两名额外的工程师，这样他们就能保持精简。他说：“我们实际上希望招聘两名全职员工，这将把我们带到甲级联赛，我们已经与投资者非常明确地谈到了这一点。”
作为拉丁裔移民的创始人，他们希望建立一家多元化和包容性的公司。他说，对他和他的联合创始人来说，找到有色人种并不难，因为他们已经与他们可以利用的不同的社交网络建立了友谊。
他说：“我们的工作是继续做我们正在做的事情，即与一群不同的人做朋友，因为这是真诚的，人们绝对可以告诉你，你正在努力完成一些多样性配额，而不是当你通常是一家以多元化为导向的公司时，因为这归根结底是创始人本身。”
这两位创始人和他们的家人从小就是朋友。他们在多米尼加共和国长大，没有机会上计算机科学课，但他们确实可以上网，他们通过阅读像这样的美国科技出版物而感染了创业漏洞，并从YouTube视频和StackOverflow中学会了编程。他们都是在美国上的大学，都在大公司实习-Facebook的Pladevall和纽约的Portet在Metadata工作。
之所以有这个想法，是因为Pladevalll是Facebook团队的一员，该团队构建了一个类似的工具供内部使用。他认为，对于没有Facebook资源的公司来说，这将是一个可行的商业想法。随着大流行的来袭，他和儿时的朋友走到了一起，并于1月份开始创建公司。
他承认今年的困难，但他说，这真的帮助他们集中精力，因为没有其他事情可做。虽然他们对银行里有260万美元感到惊讶，但他说，他们仍然有一种渴望，他认为这是移民创始人精神的一部分。
“我只是渴望证明自己，如果编码是必需的，那就学习如何编码吧。”如果是通过早期签证程序，顺便说一句，这比筹集数百万美元和通过YC要难得多，在我看来，(你可以这么做)。他说要不惜一切代价。
随着这两位朋友迈出公司的第一步，他们拥有了一些早期客户，并不断完善产品。有了今天的资金，他们对下一年有了一些远大的目标，其中包括打造该产品，达到100万美元的ARR，以及建立仪表盘的分销。
普拉德瓦尔说，如果他们能实现这些目标，他们应该能拿到甲级联赛冠军。我不会打赌他们不会这么做。
note_en:
Today they get to keep building on that dream with the help of a $2.6 million seed investment from David Sacks, Slack, Gradient Ventures,  Y Combinator   and other individual investors.
 What has attracted this investment is a couple of young founders who are passionate about making it simple to build a data dashboard without help from experts like engineers or data analysts.
  “Essentially what we do is we help companies build their business metrics dashboards with as little code or technical knowledge as possible. The byproduct of that is that anyone in the company can build their own metrics for their teams,” co-founder Xavier Pladevall told TechCrunch.
 End users can connect to a growing list of data sources and  Index   deals with building the queries and displaying the data for the users without data scientists or data analysts to help. For now, that includes Salesforce and Hubspot for CRM data, Stripe payments data and certain databases from Postgres and MongoDB.
  As the founders build out the product, they want to stay lean with just the two founders and perhaps two additional engineers. “We’re actually looking to hire two people full time, and that’s going to take us to the Series A, and we’ve been very clear with investors about that,” he said.
 As Latino immigrant founders, they want to build a company that’s diverse and inclusive. He says that’s it’s not hard for him and his co-founder to find people of color because they have formed friendships with a diverse  network of people they can tap into.
 “Our job is to keep doing what we’re doing, which is to be friends with a bunch of different people because that is genuine and people can definitely tell you’re trying to meet some diversity quota versus when you’re generally a diversity-oriented type of company because it comes back to the founders themselves,” he said.
  The two founders and their families have been friends since they were children. Growing up in the Dominican Republic, they didn’t have access to computer science classes, but they did have access to the internet and they got the startup bug from reading U.S. tech publications like this one, and learned to code from YouTube videos and StackOverflow. They both came to college in the U.S. and both interned at large companies — Pladevall at Facebook and Portet worked at Metadata in New York.
 The idea came together because Pladevalll was part of a team at Facebook building a similar tool for internal use. He decided that it would be a viable commercial idea for companies without the resources of Facebook. He came together with his childhood friend and began building the company in January as the pandemic hit.
 He acknowledges the hardship of this year, but says it really helped them focus because there wasn’t anything else to do. While they are amazed at having $2.6 million in the bank, he says they still have the hunger that he believes is part of the immigrant founder ethos.
 “It’s just hunger to just prove yourself and if coding is what it takes, learn how to code. If it’s going through an early visa process, which is by the way, way harder than raising millions of dollars and going through YC, in my opinion, [you do that]” he said. He said it’s about doing whatever it takes.
 As the two friends take their first steps as a company, they have some early customers and continue to refine the product. With today’s funding they have some lofty goals for the next year, which include building out that product, reaching $1 million in ARR and building distribution for the dashboard.
 If they can meet those goals, Pladevall says, they should be able to get their Series A. I wouldn’t bet against them.
title: 索引NABS$260万种子，用于创建无编码的BI仪表板
title_en: Index nabs $2.6M seed to create BI dashboards without coding

note:
使用粉丝徽章赚钱的想法并不是Instagram独有的。包括Twitch和YouTube在内的其他直播平台也有类似的系统。Facebook Live还允许粉丝在直播视频中购买明星，作为一种虚拟的小费机制。
Instagram用户将在直播视频中看到三种购买徽章的选择：售价0.99美元、1.99美元或4.99美元的徽章。
Instagram表示，在Instagram Live上，徽章不仅会引起人们对粉丝评论的关注，还会释放出特殊的功能。这包括在创建者的徽章持有者名单上放置一个位置，以及获得一个特殊的心形徽章。
徽章和名单让创作者更容易快速看到哪些粉丝支持他们的努力，如果需要的话，还可以给他们大喊一声。
Instagram表示，为了启动徽章的推出，它还将从11月开始，暂时匹配创作者在直播视频中购买徽章的收入。创建者@ronneBrown和@Young gezee都在测试徽章的人之列。
该公司表示，它在发布时不会收取收入份额，但随着徽章测试的扩大，它将在未来探索收入份额。
“创作者推动文化向前发展。Instagram首席运营官贾斯汀·奥索夫斯基在一份声明中表示：“他们中的许多人都为此奉献了一生，这对我们来说非常重要，他们有很容易的方式从内容中赚钱。”“这些都是我们工作中的额外步骤，目的是让Instagram成为创作者讲述故事、扩大受众和谋生的最佳场所，”她补充道。
此外，Instagram今天正在将其IGTV广告测试的访问范围扩大到更多的创作者。今年春天推出的这项计划，允许创作者通过在视频中添加广告来赚钱。Instagram说，如今，创作者至少保留了55%的收入。
此前曾宣布推出徽章和IGTV广告，Instagram表示将在今年早些时候与一小群创作者测试前者。
在这些变化之前，Instagram的直播视频平台经历了一段时间的快速增长，在冠状病毒大流行期间，创作者和粉丝在家里避难，取消了现场活动、大型聚会、音乐会等。
例如，在疫情爆发期间，Instagram表示，从2020年2月到3月，Live创作者的视频浏览量增加了70%。在第二季度，Facebook还报告了月度活跃用户增长(从第一季度的29.9亿增长到31.4亿)，称这反映了更多时间呆在家里的消费者参与度的增加。
note_en:
The idea to monetize using fan badges is not unique to Instagram. Other live streaming platforms, including  Twitch and  YouTube, have similar systems.  Facebook Live also allows fans to purchase stars on live videos, as a virtual tipping mechanism.
 Instagram users will see three options to purchase a badge during live videos: badges that cost $0.99, $1.99, or $4.99.
  On Instagram Live, badges will not only call attention to the fans’ comments, they also unlock special features, Instagram says. This includes a placement on a creator’s list of badge holders and access to a special heart badge.
 The badges and list make it easier for creators to quickly see which fans are supporting their efforts, and give them a shout-out, if desired.
  To kick off the roll out of badges, Instagram says it will also temporarily match creator earnings from badge purchases during live videos, starting in November.  Creators    @ronnebrown and  @youngezee are among those who are testing badges.
 The company says it’s not taking a revenue share at launch, but as it expands its test of badges it will explore revenue share in the future.
 “Creators push culture forward. Many of them dedicate their life to this, and it’s so important to us that they have easy ways to make money from their content,” said Instagram COO Justin Osofsky, in a statement. “These are additional steps in our work to make Instagram the single best place for creators to tell their story, grow their audience, and make a living,” she added.
 Additionally, Instagram today is expanding access to  its IGTV ads test to more creators. This program,  introduced this spring, allows creators to earn money by including ads alongside their videos. Today, creators keep at least 55% of that revenue, Instagram says.
 The introduction of badges and IGTV ads were  previously announced, with Instagram saying it would test the former with a small group of creators earlier this year.
 The changes follow what’s been a period of rapid growth on Instagram’s live video platform, as creators and fans sheltered at home during the coronavirus pandemic, which had cancelled live events, large meetups, concerts, and more.
 During the pandemic’s start, for example, Instagram said Live creators saw a 70% increase in video views from Feb. to March, 2020. In Q2,  Facebook also reported monthly active user growth (from 2.99B to 3.14B in Q1) that it said reflected increased engagement from consumers who were spending more time at home.
title: Instagram为直播视频推出粉丝徽章，扩大IGTV广告测试
title_en: Instagram rolls out fan badges for live videos, expands IGTV ads test

华盛顿--据司法部官员称，司法部计划在周二提起的诉讼中指控谷歌(Google)在搜索和搜索广告领域保持非法垄断地位，这是美国政府在一代人时间内对科技公司市场力量发起的最重大的法律挑战。
这些官员表示，在将向华盛顿特区一家联邦法院提起的诉讼中，该机构将指控Alphabet旗下的谷歌(Google)通过几份将竞争排除在外的排他性商业合同和协议，非法维持其对搜索的垄断。这些官员没有获得公开发言的授权。这类合同包括谷歌向苹果支付数十亿美元，将谷歌搜索引擎设置为iPhone的默认搜索引擎。
该机构将辩称，控制着美国约80%搜索查询的谷歌，与使用Alphabet Android操作系统的手机制造商达成了协议，在他们的手机上预装搜索引擎，使竞争对手的搜索引擎很难成为替代品。诉讼称，通过使用合同来维持其垄断地位，竞争和创新受到了损害。
这起诉讼反映了对美国最大公司权力的抵制，特别是谷歌、亚马逊、Facebook和苹果等科技巨头。特朗普总统等保守派人士和参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦(Elizabeth Warren)等自由派人士一直强烈批评权力集中在少数科技巨头手中。
由特朗普任命的司法部长威廉·P·巴尔(William P.Barr)在调查中发挥了异常积极的作用。他敦促司法部的职业律师在9月底之前提起诉讼，这引发了那些希望获得更多时间并抱怨政治影响力的律师的抵制。几个月来，巴尔一直公开谈论这项调查，并为领导调查的检察官设定了严格的最后期限。
这起诉讼可能会持续数年，并可能引发州总检察长提起的一连串其他反垄断诉讼。大约有40多个州和司法管辖区进行了平行调查，预计将分别对该公司控制在线广告技术的行为提出投诉。
政府的胜利可能会重塑美国最知名的公司之一和互联网经济，自1998年由两名斯坦福大学(Stanford University)研究生创立以来，政府一直在帮助定义互联网经济。
但谷歌长期以来一直否认违反垄断的指控，预计将利用律师、经济学家和游说者组成的全球网络来对抗政府的努力。Alphabet价值1.04万亿美元，拥有1200亿美元的现金储备，在欧洲也打过类似的反垄断诉讼。
该公司表示，随着越来越多的人在亚马逊等网站上查找信息，它在搜索市场上的竞争非常激烈。该公司表示，其服务对小企业来说是一种恩惠。
两周前，众议院司法委员会(House Judiciary Committee)的民主党议员发布了一份关于科技巨头的长篇报告，指控谷歌控制在线搜索和用户输入查询时出现的广告的垄断。
报告称：“相当多的实体--包括大型上市公司、小企业和企业家--依赖谷歌的流量，没有其他搜索引擎可以替代。”议员们还指责苹果、亚马逊和Facebook滥用其市场支配力。
这次审查反映了谷歌在过去20年里是如何成为通信、商业和媒体领域的主导参与者的。据估计，它控制着90%的在线搜索市场。这项业务利润丰厚：根据研究公司eMarketer的数据，去年，谷歌在美国带来了343亿美元的搜索收入。该公司表示，预计到2022年，这一数字将增长到425亿美元。
这起诉讼是一项持续了一年多的调查的结果。检察官已经与谷歌在技术和媒体领域的竞争对手进行了交谈，收集了可能用于立案的信息和文件。
巴尔曾是一名电信高管，曾在最高法院就一起反垄断案进行辩论。他表示，将在2019年初的确认听证会上对科技巨头进行新的审查。他说，“很多人想知道，硅谷现在存在的如此巨大的庞然大物是如何在反垄断执法者的眼皮底下形成的。”
他将调查置于他的副手杰弗里·罗森(Jeffrey Rosen)的控制之下，后者反过来从一家大型律师事务所聘请了一名助手来监督此案和其他技术事务。当司法部反垄断部门负责人马坎·德拉希姆(Makan Delrahim)回避调查时，巴尔加强了对调查的控制，因为他曾代表谷歌在2007年收购广告服务公司DoubleClick。
巴尔敦促检察官在选举日之前结束调查，并决定是否提起诉讼。虽然司法部官员通常在立案之前对他们的调查守口如瓶，但巴尔公开宣布，他打算在夏末之前就谷歌事件做出决定。当被问及未经证实的保守派言论在网上被扼杀的指控时，他提到了该机构的反垄断调查。
今年，负责此案的大约40名律师中的大多数人说，他们反对在巴尔的9月1日之前提起诉讼。30日截止日期。一些人表示，他们不会在起诉书上签名，有几个人在今年夏天离开了案件。
谷歌上一次面临美国反垄断监管机构的严格审查是在近十年前，当时联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)调查谷歌是否在搜索市场上滥用权力。根据华尔街日报报导的一份备忘录，该机构的工作人员建议对该公司提起诉讼。但该机构的五名委员在2013年投票决定不提起诉讼。
其他国家的政府对大型科技公司采取了更积极的态度。近年来，欧盟对谷歌提起了三起反垄断诉讼，重点是其搜索引擎、广告业务和Android移动操作系统。英国和澳大利亚的监管机构正在调查数字广告市场，调查最终可能会牵涉到该公司。
司法部反垄断部门前负责人比尔·贝尔(Bill Baer)表示：“这是自90年代末微软案以来，政府提起的最具新闻价值的垄断行动。”“这一点很重要，因为政府认为一个非常成功的科技平台从事了非法维护其垄断权的行为，从而损害了消费者和竞争。”
谷歌及其盟友可能会批评这起诉讼是出于政治动机。特朗普政府抨击拥有YouTube的谷歌(Google)和其他在线平台公司，称其有悖于保守派观点。
这起诉讼的持续时间可能会超过特朗普政府本身。政府起诉微软的案件花了十多年才了结。
虽然新一届民主党政府可能会审查此案背后的战略，但一些专家表示，在新领导层的领导下，不太可能撤回这一战略。
note_en:
WASHINGTON — The Justice Department plans to accuse Google of maintaining an illegal monopoly over search and search advertising in a lawsuit to be filed on Tuesday, the government’s most significant legal challenge to a tech company’s market power in a generation, according to officials at the agency.
 In its suit, to be filed in a federal court in Washington, D.C., the agency will accuse Google, a unit of Alphabet, of illegally maintaining its monopoly over search through several exclusive business contracts and agreements that lock out competition, said the officials, who were not authorized to speak on the record. Such contracts include Google’s payment of billions of dollars to Apple to place the Google search engine as the default for iPhones.
 The agency will argue that Google, which controls about 80 percent of search queries in the United States, struck agreements with phone makers using Alphabet’s Android operating system to pre-load the search engine on their phones and make it hard for rival search engines to become a replacement. By using contracts to maintain its monopoly, competition and innovation has suffered, the suit with argue.
 The suit reflects the pushback against the power of the nation’s largest corporations, and especially technology giants like Google, Amazon, Facebook and Apple. Conservatives like President Trump and liberals like Senator Elizabeth Warren have been highly critical of the concentration of power in a handful of tech behemoths.
 Attorney General William P. Barr, who was appointed by Mr. Trump, has played an unusually active role in the investigation. He pushed career Justice Department attorneys to bring the case by the end of September, prompting pushback from lawyers who wanted more time and complained of political influence. Mr. Barr has spoken publicly about the inquiry for months and set tight deadlines for the prosecutors leading the effort.
 The lawsuit  may stretch on for years and could  set off a cascade of other antitrust lawsuits from state attorneys general. About four dozen states and jurisdictions have conducted parallel investigations and are expected to bring separate complaints against the company’s grip on technology for online advertising.
 A victory for the government could remake one of America’s most recognizable companies and the internet economy that it has helped define since it was founded by two Stanford University graduate students in 1998.
 But Google has long denied accusations of antitrust violations and is expected to fight the government’s efforts by using a global network of lawyers, economists and lobbyists. Alphabet, valued at $1.04 trillion and with cash reserves of $120 billion, has fought similar antitrust lawsuits in Europe.
 The company says it has strong competition in the search market, with more people finding information on sites like Amazon. It says its services have been a boon for small businesses.
 The lawsuit comes two weeks after Democratic lawmakers on the  House Judiciary Committee released a sprawling report on the tech giants that accused Google of controlling a monopoly over online search and the ads that come up when users enter a query.
 “A significant number of entities — spanning major public corporations, small businesses and entrepreneurs — depend on Google for traffic, and no alternate search engine serves as a substitute,” the report said. The lawmakers also accused Apple, Amazon and Facebook of abusing their market power.
 The scrutiny reflects how Google has become a dominant player in communications, commerce and media over the last two decades. It controls 90 percent of the market for online searches, according to one estimate. That business is lucrative: Last year, Google brought in $34.3 billion in search revenue in the United States, according to the research firm eMarketer. That figure is expected to grow to $42.5 billion by 2022, the firm said.
 The lawsuit is the result of an investigation that has stretched for more than a year. Prosecutors have spoken with Google’s rivals in technology and media, collecting information and documents that could be used to build a case.
 Mr. Barr, a former telecom executive who once argued an antitrust case before the Supreme Court, signaled that he would put the tech giants under new scrutiny at his confirmation hearing in early 2019. He said that “a lot of people wonder how such huge behemoths that now exist in Silicon Valley have taken shape under the nose of the antitrust enforcers.”
 He put the investigation under the control of his deputy, Jeffrey Rosen, who in turn hired an aide from a major law firm to oversee the case and other technology matters. Mr. Barr’s grip over the investigation tightened when the head of the Justice Department’s antitrust division, Makan Delrahim, recused himself from the investigation because he represented Google in its acquisition of the ad service DoubleClick in 2007.
  Mr. Barr pushed prosecutors to wrap up their inquiries — and decide whether to bring a case — before Election Day. While Justice Department officials are usually tight-lipped about their investigations until a case is filed, Mr. Barr publicly declared his intention to make a decision on the Google matter by the end of the summer. He mentioned the agency’s antitrust investigation when asked about unproven charges that conservative speech is stifled online.
 This year, most of the roughly 40 lawyers building the case said they opposed bringing a complaint by Mr. Barr’s Sept. 30 deadline. Some said they would not sign the complaint, and several left the case this summer.
 Google last faced serious scrutiny from an American antitrust regulator nearly a decade ago, when the Federal Trade Commission investigated whether it had abused its power over the search market. The agency’s staff recommended bringing charges against the company, according to a memo  reported on by The Wall Street Journal. But the agency’s five commissioners  voted in 2013 not to bring a case.
 Other governments have been more aggressive against the big tech companies. The European Union has brought three antitrust cases against Google in recent years, focused on its search engine, advertising business and Android mobile operating system. Regulators in Britain and Australia are examining the digital advertising market, in inquiries that could ultimately implicate the company.
 “It’s the most newsworthy monopolization action brought by the government since the Microsoft case in the late ‘90s,” said Bill Baer, a former chief of the Justice Department’s antitrust division. “It’s significant in that the government believes that a highly successful tech platform has engaged in conduct that maintains its monopoly power unlawfully, and as a result injures consumers and competition.”
 Google and its allies will likely criticize the suit as being politically motivated. The Trump administration has attacked Google, which owns YouTube, and other online platform companies, as being slanted against conservative views.
 The lawsuit will likely outlast the Trump administration itself. The government’s case against Microsoft took more than a decade to settle.
 While it is possible that a new Democratic administration would review the strategy behind the case, some experts said it was unlikely that it would be withdrawn under new leadership.
title: 美国将指控谷歌保护非法垄断
title_en: US To Accuse Google of Protecting Illegal Monopoly

握手为大学年龄的学生提供了一个平台，让他们登记自己的兴趣和技能，寻找合适的工作，招聘人员也可以搜索候选人，发布入门级职位空缺的广告。在一轮增长融资中，该公司已经筹集了8000万美元。
握手没有透露其估值，但一位接近这家初创公司的可靠消息人士表示，自上一轮融资以来，估值已经翻了一番以上。当时的估值为2.75亿美元，现在可能的估值在5.5亿至6亿美元之间。
该公司自2014年成立以来，在一定程度上建立了自己的形象，部分原因是它是一个更具包容性的LinkedIn版本，针对的是刚刚开始进入就业市场的人，它正在利用这笔资金加倍努力。
它现在覆盖了1,700万名求职者、1,000所高等教育机构和近50万名雇主，并与大约120家为少数族裔服务的机构建立了合作伙伴关系，其中包括历史上一直是黑人的学院和大学，以及美国的拉美裔服务机构，以帮助它们和他们的学生更好地应对求职招聘市场。
今年，握手一直在利用其最新的资金-实际上已于2019年11月关闭-扩大到其网络中也包括社区大学，并扩大其虚拟活动服务。
D系列由GGV牵头，还包括所有现有投资者的参与。握手已经有了一批杰出的支持者：它的上一轮融资是在2018年进行的4000万美元的C系列融资，由EQT牵头，还包括Chan Zuckerberg Initiative、Omidyar Network和REACH Capital，以及True Ventures、凯鹏华盈(Kleiner Perkins)、LightSpeed Venture Partners、星火资本(Spark Capital)和KPCB Edge。
握手公司首席执行官加勒特·洛德与斯科特·林韦尔斯基(CTO)和本·克里斯滕森(董事会成员)共同创立了这家公司，他表示，冠状病毒不仅影响了就业市场，也影响了求职市场。
他说：“正如你可以想象的那样，这场流感大流行真的重塑了招聘经济。”“公司不能再去校园招聘了”--传统上，公司与刚进入就业市场的人建立联系的方式很大一部分是通过活动，让他们可以集体会见许多人--“因此，我们看到了前所未有的虚拟招聘转变。”
他补充说，“在Covid出现之前”，虚拟活动变得越来越受欢迎，但突然之间，它成了镇上唯一的游戏。他说，目前约有2万名雇主使用握手平台在机构管理虚拟招聘活动。这些活动采取在线交流会和交易会的形式，提供5次30分钟的小组会议，每次最多50名学生，由招聘人员提供演示并与学生交谈；和/或与学生进行10分钟的1：1会议，最多15名招聘人员。
一切都很好，除了就业市场本身仍然不稳定。洛德说，在疫情爆发之初，招聘人数下降了20-30%，酒店业等特定行业领跌，那些仍在招聘的行业放弃了积极主动的校园招聘。现在，七个月过去了，其中许多人意识到，他们必须继续保持可见性，并正在慢慢回归。
洛德说：“他们比以往任何时候都更需要握手，用数字化和身临其境的体验取代地面体验。”
在管理宏观经济收缩的同时，今年将社区学院纳入握手范围是一件巨大的事情。
长期以来，人们一直认为两年制和四年制院校之间存在着声望和专业知识的差异，但随着我们对高等教育概念的不断发展，许多学生完全放弃了大学，或者选择了不能延伸到大学或学院四年学习的职业学位，大学变得越来越昂贵，是时候让帮助一层学生的平台也帮助另一层学生了。
对于它的投资者来说，当公司不仅在谈论想要建立更多样化的劳动力队伍，而是把钱放在嘴边，并将这种变化内在化是你有时需要积极主动才能实现的事情时，握手是一家令人信服的初创公司，值得投资。
GGV管理合伙人杰夫·理查兹在一份声明中表示：“自成立以来，握手一直专注于通过将雇主与高等教育机构的求职学生联系起来，实现其实现就业机会民主化的愿景，并建立了一个由1700万名求职者、1000所高等院校和近50万名雇主组成的丰富网络，”GGV管理合伙人杰夫·理查兹(Jeff Richards)在一份声明中表示。我们很高兴与握手团队联手，通过提供创新的、行业领先的招聘解决方案并向新市场扩张，帮助公司进一步扩大影响力。“
note_en:
Handshake, which provides a platform for college-aged students to register their interest and skills and search for suitable work, and for recruiters to search for candidates and advertise entry-level openings, has raised $80 million in a growth round of funding.
 Handshake   is not disclosing its valuation but a reliable source close to the startup said that the valuation has more than doubled since its last round. That was at $275 million, putting the likely valuation now between $550 million and $600 million.
  The company has been around since 2014 and has built its profile in part as a more inclusive version of LinkedIn aimed at people only start starting out in the job market, and it’s using the funding to double down on that.
 It now covers 17 million job seekers, 1,000 institutions of higher learning and nearly 500,000 employers, with partnerships with some 120 minority-serving institutions, which include Historically Black Colleges and Universities, and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the U.S., to help them and their students better tackle the job-hunting-recruitment market.
 And in this year, Handshake has been using its latest funding — which actually closed in November 2019 — to expand to also including community colleges in its network, and expand its  virtual events services.
 The Series D is being led by GGV and also includes participation from all of its existing investors. Handshake already had an illustrious list of backers: its last round, a  $40 million Series C in 2018, was led by EQT and also included the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, Omidyar Network and Reach Capital, as well as True Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Spark Capital and KPCB Edge.
 Garrett Lord, Handshake’s CEO who co-founded the company with Scott Ringwelski (CTO) and Ben Christensen (a board member), said that the coronavirus has not just impacted the job market, but also the job-hunting market.
 “The pandemic, as you can imagine, has really reshaped the hiring economy,” he said. “Companies can no longer go to campus to recruit” — traditionally a huge part of how companies connect with those just entering the job market, by way of events where they can meet many people en masse — “so we’ve seen an unprecedented shift to virtual recruiting.”
 Virtual events had, he added, been gaining more popularity “prior to Covid,” but suddenly it became the only game in town. He said that currently some 20,000 employers have managed virtual recruitment events at institutions using the Handshake platform. These take the form of online mixers and fairs, where it provides five 30-minute group meetings with up to 50 students in each, with recruiters providing presentations and talking with students; and/or 10-minute 1:1 meetings with students with up to 15 recruiters.
 All well and good, except that the job market itself is still rocky. Lord said that there was a 20-30% drop in listings at the start of the pandemic, with particular sectors like hospitality leading that decline, with those still hiring pulling away from proactive campus recruitment. Now, seven months on, many of those realize that they have to continue to be visible and are slowly coming back.
 “They need Handshake more than ever before, to replace boots on ground experience with digital and immersive experiences,” Lord said.
 While managing the macroeconomic contraction, the expansion this year to including community colleges on Handshake has been a huge deal.
 There has long been a perceived prestige and expertise divide between 2-year and 4-year institutions, but as our concept of higher education continues to evolve, with many students foregoing college altogether, or opting for vocational degrees that do not extend to four years of study at a university or college, and college becomes ever more expensive, it’s about time that platforms that are helping one tier of students also helps the other.
 And for its investors, at a time when companies are not just talking about wanting to build more diverse work forces, but putting money where their mouths are, and internalizing that change is something that you sometimes need to be proactive to effect, Handshake is a compelling startup to invest in.
 “Since its founding, Handshake has been laser focused on delivering on its vision to democratize job opportunity by connecting employers with job seeking students at institutions of higher education, and has built a rich network of 17 million job seekers, 1,000 institutions of higher learning and nearly 500,000 employers,” said Jeff Richards, Managing Partner of GGV, in a statement. “We’re delighted to join forces with the Handshake team to help the company further expand its impact by delivering innovative, industry-leading recruitment solutions and expanding into new markets.”
title: 握手是一款面向大学生的多元化LinkedIn，由GGV牵头筹集了8000万美元的D系列资金，消息人士称，估值在5.5亿-6亿美元之间
title_en: Handshake, a diversity-focused LinkedIn for college students, raises $80M Series D led by GGV, source says at a $550M-$600M valuation

微软将于10月20日推出Azure Space计划。Azure Space是一套产品，加上新宣布的合作伙伴关系，旨在将Azure定位为云市场中与空间和卫星相关的连接/计算领域的关键参与者。“蔚蓝空间”并不是航天工业公司的专利。它的目的是吸引农业、能源、电信和政府市场的公共和私营行业客户的公司。它也适用于任何有远程访问和带宽需求的客户。微软的主要云竞争对手AWS在2020年6月宣布了它自己的空间产业战略和空间部门，名为航空航天和卫星解决方案。它还创建了自己的卫星连接服务，AWS地面站，以及一家名为Project Kuiper的卫星企业，与SpaceX的Starlink和其他卫星网络提供商展开竞争。正如微软在AWS拥有自己的产品和服务的其他领域所做的那样，这些产品和服务可能会与客户的产品和服务展开竞争。微软正在夸大这样一个事实，即它自己并没有试图成为一家卫星供应商。取而代之的是，将继续与卫星公司合作，开展其Azure Space努力。为了强调这一信息，微软正在兜售埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)的Space X，将其作为Azure Space的重要合作伙伴之一。微软正在与SpaceX合作，在Azure上提供卫星供电的互联网连接。这两家公司计划提供将SpaceX的Starlink卫星宽带连接到微软新的Azure模块化数据中心的选项。SpaceX本周刚刚宣布，它又发射了60颗Starlink卫星，用于低地球轨道部署，这是其卫星宽带服务公开测试版的一部分。Azure Modular Datacenter(MDC)也在今天，也就是10月20日发布，它是一个装运集装箱的Azure(也就是可现场运输的解决方案)。MDC--包括其自己的HVAC系统、服务器机架、网络和安全功能--旨在为客户在远程位置设置Azure数据中心提供更坚固的选择。MDC可以连接或断开运行。目前，MDC运行Azure Stack Hub，但一位微软发言人表示，未来可能不会这样，因为MDC是一个独立的数据中心。(也许这是对Azure Stack斐济的认可，微软仍然是AWS前哨的非官方竞争对手？微软和SpaceX还计划在未来提供Starlink和微软全球网络(包括Azure边缘计算设备)之间的连接。其想法是将SpaceX的地面站与Azure联网功能相结合，让客户能够获得从机器学习和可视化到生产力服务的各种微软服务。微软正在吹捧参与其Azure Space计划的其他合作伙伴包括卫星运营商SES、KSAT、Viasat、Kratos、amergint、KubOS和US Electrdynamic。微软上个月在其Ignite大会上宣布与SES for Azure Orbital建立合作伙伴关系；两家公司还计划开展更多合作，以扩大与MDC和其他云数据中心区域和设备的卫星连接。Azure Orbary是一项新服务，将为卫星运营商提供物理卫星通信功能。通过私人预览，轨道公司将使卫星运营商能够在Azure中处理和分析数据，并安排对Azure轨道地面站天线的访问。去年，微软在Ignite上宣布了一项名为“卫星快速路线”的相关服务。这项服务面向企业客户，而不是卫星运营商，使用户能够通过私有和专用连接从远程站点通信到Azure位置。这两项服务现在都是Azure Space产品组合的一部分。
微软今天还宣布了一项名为Azure Orbital Emulator的新的相关服务。Azure轨道仿真器是一个仿真环境，用软件和硬件进行大规模的卫星星座仿真。它适用于卫星开发人员，他们需要在发射卫星之前评估和培训涉及卫星联网的人工智能算法。官员们说，Azure Orbital Emulator已经在微软的Azure政务云中被客户使用。
note_en:
Microsoft is launching its  Azure Space initiative on October 20. Azure Space is a set of products, plus newly announced partnerships designed to position Azure as a key player in the space- and satellite-related connectivity/compute part of the cloud market.     Azure Space isn't just for companies in the space industry. It's meant to appeal to companies in public and private industry customers in the agriculture, energy, telecommunications, and government markets. It's also meant for any customer with remote-access and bandwidth needs.     Microsoft's main cloud rival,  AWS, announced its own space-industry strategy and space unit called Aerospace and Satellite Solutions, in June 2020. It also has created its own satellite connection service, AWS Ground Station, and a  satellite venture called Project Kuiper which competes with SpaceX's Starlink and other satellite networking providers.     As it has done in other areas where AWS has its own products and services that could potentially compete with those from customers, Microsoft is playing up the fact that it isn't trying to be a satellite provider itself. Instead, will continue to partner with satellite companies with its Azure Space effort.     To hammer home this message, Microsoft is touting Elon Musk's Space X as one of its marquee Azure Space partners. Microsoft is working with SpaceX to provide satellite-powered Internet connectivity on Azure. The pair plan to deliver the option to connect SpaceX's Starlink satellite broadband to Microsoft's new Azure Modular Datacenter. SpaceX just announced this week that it has  launched 60 more Starlink satellites for low-Earth orbit deployment as part of its gearing up for a  public beta of its satellite-broadband service.     The  Azure Modular Datacenter (MDC), also announced today, October 20, is Azure in a shipping container (a k a, a "field-transportable" solution"). The MDC -- which includes its own HVAC system, server racks, networking and security capabilities -- is meant to give customers a ruggedized option for setting up an Azure datacenter in remote locations. The MDC can run connected or disconnected. For now, MDC runs Azure Stack Hub, but a Microsoft spokesperson said this might not be the case in the future, as MDC is a self-contained datacenter. (Maybe that's a nod to  Azure Stack Fiji, Microsoft's still non-officially-announced competitor to AWS Outposts? Not sure.)     Microsoft and SpaceX also plan, in the future, to offer connections between Starlink and Microsoft's global network, including Azure edge-computing devices. The idea is to integrate SpaceX's ground stations with Azure networking capabilities, giving customers access to all kinds of Microsoft services, ranging from machine-learning and visualization, to productivity services.     Other partners Microsoft is touting as participating in its Azure Space initiative include satellite operator SES, KSAT, Viasat, Kratos, Amergint, KubOS and US Electrodynamics. Microsoft announced a partnership with SES for Azure Orbital last month at its Ignite conference; the pair also plan to do more work together to expand satellite connectivity with the MDC and other cloud datacenter regions and devices.      Azure Orbital is a new service that will provide access to physical satellite communications capabilities to satellite operators. Via private preview, Orbital will enable satellite operators to process and analyze data in Azure and schedule access to Azure Orbital ground station antennas. Last year at Ignite, Microsoft announced a related service called  ExpressRoute for Satellite. This service, aimed at enterprise customers, not satellite operators, enabled users to communicate from a remote site to Azure locations over private and dedicated connections. Both of these services are now part of the Azure Space portfolio.
 Microsoft also announced today a new related service called Azure Orbital Emulator. Azure Orbital Emulator is an emulation environment that conducts massive satellite constellation simulations with software and hardware. It's for satellite developers who need to evaluate and train AI algorithms involving satellite networking before launching satellites. Azure Orbital Emulator is already being used by customers in Microsoft's Azure Government cloud, officials said.
title: 微软与SpaceX合作，在Azure上实现卫星供电的互联网连接，并首次亮相Azure Space，这是一套面向与空间相关的云市场的产品
title_en: Microsoft partners with SpaceX for satellite-powered internet connectivity on Azure and debuts Azure Space, a set of products for the space-related cloud market

今年是iPhone的重要一年：苹果的iPhone12系列完全重新设计，有四个型号-iPhone12 Mini，iPhone12，iPhone12 Pro和iPhone12 Pro Max-在不同的屏幕尺寸和价位范围内。苹果全面增加了新的视频功能，新的MagSafe充电系统，新的A14处理器，以及它可以为5G聚集的所有炒作。
很明显，Mini和Max iPhone12s的区别是显而易见的，但中间两款6.1英寸的机型非常相似。IPhone 12和iPhone 12 Pro拥有相同的基本设计、非常相似的OLED显示屏、相同的处理器和5G功能。Pro增加了一个额外的长焦镜头，一个激光雷达传感器，稍微多一点的RAM，两倍的基本存储空间，以及一个闪亮的不锈钢边框。所有这些都要花999美元，比运营商补贴价格799美元的基本款iPhone12贵200美元左右。
现在，你们中的一些人会花掉多余的钱，因为这是闪闪发光的。在大多数情况下，我会做出同样的选择，因为我已经开始接受自己的弱点。但值得一试，看看额外的钱是否值得，特别是因为iPhone 12现在有OLED显示屏，这意味着普通iPhone和Pro之间的差异从根本上比去年小得多，当时普通机型的LCD分辨率较低。
因此，iPhone 12 Pro面临的真正问题是，这一小部分额外功能是否值得比标准iPhone 12高出大约200美元。如果你的花费要高得多，那么再等一段时间，再花100美元购买iPhone 12 Pro Max可能是值得的，它将增加更大的显示屏和更大的主摄像头传感器，并配备非常耐人寻味的新传感器偏移稳定系统，可以大幅提升画质。
这使得12Pro处于一个奇怪的位置，真的，我认为这归根结底是你可能会在晚上使用长焦镜头或拍摄肖像照片的程度。
IPhone12和12Pro有着相同的基本新设计，它非常平方，非常平坦--几乎是令人震惊的平坦。几乎所有其他手机都有弯曲的边缘，显示屏和边框之间有明显的边界，但iPhone12s给人的感觉更像是一个整体。它让我有点想起iPhone5，但更重要的是，它的外观和感觉与任何其他现代手机都有很大的不同，这是苹果历来擅长让旧设计看起来立即过时的方式。
奇怪的是，平方的设计也让iPhone12 Pro看起来比实际情况更坚固。它看起来不像，但比iPhone11Pro还薄。
正如我提到的，12Pro是一款闪亮的，光滑的不锈钢边框立即捕捉到了指纹。这是一部手机，如果你不把它放在盒子里，你会经常试图保持干净。尽管如此，这也是很长一段时间以来我一直为报道感到难过的第一部iPhone。只是看起来很不错。
手机的正面覆盖着苹果公司所说的“陶瓷盾牌”，这是一种玻璃和陶瓷的混合体。在新材料(你不能称之为“玻璃”，因为从技术上讲，它不是玻璃)和新设计之间，苹果公司声称，iPhone12系列的跌落性能是之前型号的四倍，并且具有同样的耐擦伤性。(我的手机掉得很多，所以我很想看看事情进展如何。)。苹果公司表示，在背面，你会发现玻璃的类型与去年相同，但新的设计也应该会改善它的跌落性能。苹果不愿告诉我的一件事是，这款不锈钢边框对…的划痕和划痕有多大的抵抗力。我们已经在我们的审查单元的画面上划了一个小缺口，尽管它实际上所做的一切只是从一个视频拍摄到另一个视频拍摄。
IPhone 12 Pro的OLED显示屏比iPhone 11 Pro大，为6.1英寸，手机也因此略高。在亮度和像素密度方面，显示屏在其他方面与去年基本相同，这意味着它看起来很棒，尽管它的刷新率仍然是60赫兹，目前几乎所有售价在700美元以上的安卓手机都落后于这个刷新率。
苹果的销售量和可用的显示面板供应是有借口的，但归根结底，60 Hz的显示屏根本就不太…。职业选手。事实上，苹果自己的iPad Pro有一个高刷新率的促销显示屏。如果你只用过iPhone，你不会真正注意到这一点，因为它和以前一样。但是，如果您使用的是120 Hz的显示器，那么滚动时流畅度的差异肯定是显而易见的。
屏幕顶部的边框和凹槽仍然是相同的大小，这或多或少意味着你最终不会注意到它们。早期采用者会注意到，一些应用程序需要根据这种新的屏幕尺寸进行更新--Instagram像往常一样有点破旧--但iPhone生态系统通常适应得很快，所以我预计会有一系列应用程序更新。
在手机的侧面，你会发现四个标准的天线间隙，美国型号有一个用于Verizon上的超宽带(UWB)5G的毫米波(MmWave)天线窗口。苹果公司告诉我们，把手机放在窗户上不应该影响无线性能，而且没有关于如何握住手机的特别指导。我的手很大，除非我试一试，否则基本上不能拿着手机，除非我盖住天线窗口，在我简短的超宽带测试中，我没有任何问题。
手机背面采用了苹果全新的MagSafe磁性无线充电和挂载系统，感觉就像是第一次上大学前的那个夏天的连接器：未来是光明和令人兴奋的，你可以从头开始重塑你的整个情况。但Lightning连接器的日子显然即将结束，对此感到悲伤也没什么。
作为一个概念，MagSafe是非常聪明的，在现实中也是非常好的。我对磁性汽车悬置和稳定器万向节和其他不需要小提琴就能连接的配件感到兴奋。我还认为在充电线圈下面会巧妙地使用额外的对准磁铁；这就是让苹果公司的Just-OK钱包套正确连接的磁铁。与iPad Smart Connector不同，苹果似乎有信心开发出一个真正的MagSafe配件生态系统。已经有了第三方充电器、车载和正在宣布的东西。(苹果公司声称，因为有磁铁，把它更标准的保护套拿到手机上和手机下更容易，我不知道他们在说什么。它看起来和以前一样。)。
撇开有趣的可能性不谈，实际的MagSafe充电盘对于你可能随身携带的东西来说是相当大的，尽管它对于无线充电器来说很小，而且我对把它放在包里旅行也不是特别兴奋。电源线本身很短，所以感觉就像是朝着一个版本迈出了很小的一步，当你在沙发上或在床上用低电池的时候，它就可以工作了。苹果还没有推出一款更长的线缆，但这似乎是不可避免的。
MagSafe也很贵。如果你想使用苹果39美元的MagSafe充电器，这样你就可以快速充电15瓦，你需要购买自己的20瓦USB-C电源适配器，或者花19美元买苹果的，因为它已经不在包装盒里了。(总体而言，今年从iPhone盒中移除电源适配器和耳机似乎是一件好事，但对环境的影响好坏参半。)。
如果你不想花那么多钱，你可以使用任何普通的齐无线充电器进行高达7.5瓦的充电，就像iPhone 11系列一样，苹果公司表示，未来第三方将能够生产经过批准的MagSafe充电器，所以希望很快就会有一些更便宜的选择。
总而言之，MagSafe是一个非常聪明的想法，在它真正取代Lightning连接器并交付苹果未来似乎正在走向的无端口iPhone之前，它需要更多的时间来烘焙。
去年我说iPhone11Pro拥有智能手机上最好的摄像头，而iPhone12Pro又不是倒退的。但这只是前进了一小步-足以保持在竞争中的微弱领先地位。大多数改进都相当小。
主摄像头的镜头比去年稍微亮了一点，这有助于它在光线较弱的情况下使用，而苹果新的Smart HDR 3处理似乎，嗯，更智能了一点。与iPhone11相比，降噪效果更好，看起来也更好：照片看起来不那么粗糙，细节也更多一些。照片的对比度也略高一些；每年，苹果似乎都更愿意让高光成为高光，让阴影成为阴影，这是我个人喜欢的样子。
手机上的四个摄像头现在都可以进行夜间模式，这是非常好的，但对于夜间模式自拍来说，前置摄像头最有用。使用电视或超宽镜头拍摄夜间模式是可行的，但我会坚持使用主相机。这是手机上最好的摄像头，可以拍到最好的图像。
与11Pro相比，11Pro还有其他一些小但值得注意的照片改进：超宽镜头的边缘扭曲稍小，超宽和长焦的照片更清晰、更详细。还有一些新的设置：你实际上可以关闭镜头校正，你可以关闭Smart HDR和“场景检测”，这两个功能试图智能地识别镜头并正确曝光。很高兴看到苹果接受了更多的摄像头选项，但我通常会让所有东西都打开；如果你对自动摄像头模式感到反感，你应该只使用像Halide这样具有良好手动控制的应用程序。
12Pro的背面也有一个新的激光雷达传感器，这是我们在iPad Pro上第一次看到的。如果你沉迷于AR的花招，你会爱上激光雷达传感器的。它使AR的噱头比以往任何时候都要好。(我不喜欢AR的花招，但我尝试的那些相当不错。)。
在常规的非增强现实中，大多数人会在微光情况下利用12 Pro上的LIDAR传感器，在这种情况下，相机可以更快地对焦-据苹果公司说，最高可快六倍-还可以拍摄夜间模式的人像照片。
在测试中，对焦确实很快，iPhone 12 Pro的微光照片比iPhone 11 Pro更清晰，因为它有更亮的镜头，可以实现更快的快门速度。
夜间模式的人像照片非常令人印象深刻-尽管它们只适用于广角镜头，而不适用于电视。谷歌的Pixel5也可以拍摄夜景肖像照片，但要做到这一点可能很麻烦。IPhone12Pro刚刚拍下了令人印象深刻的照片，不需要摆弄。
除非你非常喜欢AR的噱头或夜间模式的人像照片，否则我认为你不会从iPhone12LIDAR传感器中获得太多价值。当你在普通光线下拍照时，相机会像往常一样聚焦；激光雷达传感器没有激活。在许多方面，感觉LIDAR大部分时间都在手机上，这样苹果和其他人就可以想出未来要用它做什么。
与Google Pixel 5和三星Note 20 Ultra相比，iPhone12 Pro提供了我们对苹果的期望：几乎所有情况下都有很棒的照片，色彩平衡，细节也很棒。我仍然更喜欢Pixel的外观，而且我得到的可靠消息是，有些人更喜欢三星的超逼真颜色，但iPhone11Pro是最好的手机。IPhone12Pro在大多数情况下比11Pro略有改进，并增加了在更困难的边缘情况下获得可用镜头的能力。
悬而未决的一件大事是，我们还没有测试iPhone12Pro Max，它有一个更大的主摄像头传感器，带有新的稳定系统。12Pro上的电视镜头很不错，但苹果把超宽镜头放在普通12上是有原因的：它只是功能更多。如果你花额外的钱是因为你想在你的iPhone上买一个稍微好一点的摄像头，我会等着看多花一点钱是否能给你买到一个好得多的摄像头。正如我的同事丹·塞弗特(Dan Seifert)所说，这就是他们如何理解你的。
说到视频，iPhone12系列的另一个重要的新摄像头功能是能够创建杜比视觉HDR视频。如果不深入到视频格式的杂草中去谈论这件事是不可能的，但我向你保证这会很有趣的。我保证。
HDR代表高动态范围。你可能熟悉静止摄影中的这个术语，在那里，智能手机相机中的HDR系统是一场革命。但是要理解视频中的HDR，你必须把你从摄影HDR中了解到的一切放在一边。这是非常非常不同的。
视频中的HDR基本上意味着图像中最亮的部分可以变得更亮，与SDR或标准动态范围相比，您可以在图像的明亮部分看到更多细节。这实际上很容易在iPhone上显示。当你拍摄视频时，它会在HDR中捕捉，当你滑动相机胶卷进行回放时，你可以看到显示器改变了模式，变得更亮了。那是HDR。
HDR视频通常也有更多可用的颜色。SDR视频可以有1600万种颜色，而iPhone上的HDR视频有7亿种颜色。这是很多额外的颜色，这意味着像渐变和日落之类的东西看起来更平滑。综合起来，HDR视频比SDR视频更鲜艳、更丰富多彩，太棒了。
在过去的几年里，观看HDR视频变得非常容易。几乎每一款新的高端电视和手机都支持HDR，大多数主要的流媒体服务都提供HDR格式的电影和电视节目。(HBO Max是最引人注目的例外。)。但是，自己拍摄和分享HDR视频仍然很困难。这并非不可能：Galaxy S10之后的三星手机可以拍摄一种名为HDR10+的格式，但这是一种隐藏得很深的设置。如果你真的有动力，从索尼RX100 Mark V到Up，都有工作流程来获取HDR视频。但这一切都不容易。
因此，iPhone默认拍摄开箱即用的HDR视频是一件大事。
我一直在说“HDR”，不是“Dolby Vision”这是因为杜比视觉是一种HDR-一种格式-有很多种HDR格式。随之而来的是很多格式的兼容性问题。是的，iPhone12上的Dolby Vision存在一些兼容性问题--但没有我担心的那么多。苹果和杜比做了一些非常聪明的事情，以牺牲一些短期的不兼容性为代价，确保了长期的兼容性。
杜比视觉和竞争对手的HDR10格式是所谓的感知量化器(PQ)HDR系统。PQ系统不能向后兼容SDR显示器，这意味着Dolby Vision视频只与Dolby Vision显示器兼容。(在非Dolby Vision显示器上播放Dolby Vision视频看起来完全错误。)。如果你想在普通显示器上观看Dolby Vision的视频，你必须将其重新编码成SDR-基本上，就是制作一个全新的视频文件。
但iPhone12是用更新版本的杜比视觉(Dolby Vision)拍摄视频的，名为Profile 8.4，该版本基于一种名为HLG的标准，即混合对数伽马。HLG的工作原理与PQ系统不同；它是由BBC和NHK等广播公司开发的，向后兼容SDR显示器，因此它们只需广播一个视频流。
所有这些都意味着，iPhone12 HDR视频是一个10位HLG文件，顶部有附加的杜比视觉元数据，它将在SDR显示器上以SDR格式播放，在HLG显示器上以HLG格式播放，在支持配置文件8.4的杜比视觉显示器上以Dolby Vision格式播放。IOS14足够聪明，可以知道你分享视频的应用程序和设备何时不支持这种新格式，它会确保你发送的视频有效。这一切都是非常聪明的，尽管在实践中，这意味着你大部分时间都在分享特别提款权(SDR)视频。
这是个圈套。(当然。)。Dolby Vision Profile 8.4是新版本的Dolby Vision，这意味着需要更新一些较旧的Dolby Vision设备来支持它。因此，我不能向你保证任何带有AirPlay 2的随机杜比视觉电视都会在杜比视觉中播放iPhone12Pro视频。您必须检查一下您的电视是否支持配置文件8.4。迪特尔的老款Vizio p65有AirPlay 2，但可以用SDR播放iPhone12的杜比视觉视频。我的2016款LG B6 OLED支持杜比视觉(Dolby Vision)，但不支持Profile 8.4，而且根本不能播放U盘上的iPhone12视频文件。它现在也足够老了，可能永远也不会有另一个软件更新来做到这一点。诸若此类。
这就是说，新的电视自然会支持最新版本的杜比视觉，你可以随时插入苹果电视4K和AirPlay来实现这一点。(不过，iPhone12杜比视觉视频与苹果电视一起工作的方式是不稳定的：你必须一直在杜比视觉模式下运行苹果电视，才能让它工作。如果你是一个神智正常的人，在打开“匹配内容”的情况下使用SDR格式的Apple TV，你仍然会看到iPhone12上的SDR视频。我希望苹果能尽快解决这个问题。)。
哦，如果你有一台Mac，在MacOS Big Sur推出之前，杜比视觉视频将不会被支持，而我们真的不知道什么时候会推出。我也不知道Instagram、TikTok和YouTube等什么时候会支持来自iPhone的Dolby Vision视频；现在，当你将你的视频分享到这些平台上时，你会看到SDR格式的视频。(YouTube目前支持HDR视频，但仅支持HLG和HDR10。格式！)。
因此，iPhone上的Dolby Vision视频有点复杂，一开始会有一些兼容性问题。但随着时间的推移，我预计它会逐渐消失。毕竟，电视制造商和社交平台有很多动机来弄清楚如何很好地播放人们的iPhone视频。
与此同时，大多数人确实是在智能手机上观看智能手机视频，iPhone 12 Dolby Vision视频在iPhone 12 Pro和其他支持HDR的新款iPhone和iPad上看起来非常明亮和丰富多彩。我很兴奋它在那里，更兴奋的是它是默认的。
我有幸与Verge视频总监贝卡·法萨斯(Becca Farsace)一起做这篇评论，所以我问她对iPhone12Pro总体视频质量的一些看法。以下是她不得不说的话：
在用iPhone12Pro拍摄视频时，我注意到了三件事：极高的细节水平，夜间更明亮的镜头，以及白天的光线反射。和11Pro一样，iPhone12Pro可以在60fps和1080p的240fps下拍摄高达4K的图像。去年，iPhone11Pro的视频功能给我留下了深刻的印象，而iPhone12Pro的…也没什么不同。就像，实际上很难区分11号和12号的镜头。
在完美的照明条件下，视频非常漂亮。颜色鲜艳，细节清晰，能很好地处理肤色。12号相机的镜头在白天会稍微暖和一点，但除非你真的想找出这些相机之间的不同之处，否则你是不会找到的。
在晚上，12Pro的整体亮度稍高一些，在平滑黑暗区域以对抗噪音方面做得很好。主感应器肯定是最亮的，一旦你切换到超宽感应器，情况就会变得有点暗。但更令人担心的是，在夜间拍摄时，使用那个主感应器，画面上还是有一些小圆点！任何直接进入镜头的光线，比如来自汽车的前灯，都会将大量的光线反射到图像上，这相当分散人的注意力。这是footag的问题。

Skip to main content                                                                                                                By       @reckless        Photography by  Amelia Holowaty Krales | Video by  Becca Farsace,  Phil Esposito, and  Alix Diaconis | Sound by  Andrew Marino | Graphics by  Grayson Blackmon
 If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our  ethics statement.
    It’s a big year for the iPhone: Apple’s iPhone 12 line is totally redesigned and features four models — the iPhone 12 mini, the  iPhone 12, the iPhone 12 Pro, and the iPhone 12 Pro Max — at a range of screen sizes and price points. Across the board, Apple’s added new video features, a new MagSafe charging system, the new A14 processor, and all of the hype it can muster for 5G.
 It’s obvious what sets the mini and Max iPhone 12s apart, but the two 6.1-inch models in the middle of the line are remarkably similar. The iPhone 12 and the iPhone 12 Pro have the same basic design, very similar OLED displays, and the same processors and 5G capabilities. The Pro adds an extra telephoto camera lens, a LIDAR sensor, a little more RAM, twice the base storage, and a shiny stainless steel frame. All that will cost you $999, around $200 more than a base iPhone 12 at the carrier-subsidized price of $799.
 Now, there are some of you who are going to spend the extra money because this is the shiny one. In most cases, I would make the same choice because I have come to accept my weaknesses. But it’s worth diving in to see if the extra money is worth it, especially since the iPhone 12 now has an OLED display, which means the differences between the regular iPhone and the Pro are fundamentally much smaller than last year, when the regular model had a lower-resolution LCD.
 So the real question for the iPhone 12 Pro is whether the small list of extra features justifies a roughly $200 price bump from the standard iPhone 12. And if you’re spending that much more, it might be worth it to wait a little while longer and spend another $100 on the iPhone 12 Pro Max, which will add a bigger display and a larger main camera sensor with a very intriguing new sensor-shift stabilization system that could offer a huge jump in picture quality.
 That leaves the 12 Pro in a weird spot, and really, I think it comes down to how much you might use the telephoto lens or shoot portrait photos at night.
    The iPhone 12 and 12 Pro share the same fundamental new design, which is very squared off and very flat — almost shockingly flat. Nearly every other phone has a curved edge and an obvious border between the display and the frame, but the iPhone 12s feels much more like a single piece. It reminds me a little of the iPhone 5, but more importantly, it looks and feels very different than any other modern phone, in the way Apple is historically good at making older designs seem instantly out of date.
 Oddly, the squared-off design also makes the iPhone 12 Pro seem more substantial than it actually is. It doesn’t look it, but it’s thinner than an iPhone 11 Pro.
 As I mentioned, the 12 Pro is the shiny one, and the glossy stainless steel frame instantly picked up fingerprints. This is a phone you will be trying to keep clean quite often if you don’t put it in a case. Even still, this is the first iPhone in a long time that I’ve been sad about covering. It’s just nice to look at.
  The front of the phone is covered in what Apple calls “Ceramic Shield,” a hybrid of glass and ceramic. Between the new material — you can’t call it “glass” because it’s technically not glass — and the new design, Apple claims the iPhone 12 line has four times better drop performance than the previous models, with the same scratch resistance. (I drop my phone a lot, so I’m excited to see how this goes.) On the back, you’ll find the same type of glass as last year, but the new design should improve its drop performance as well, Apple says. One thing Apple would not tell me is how resistant this stainless steel frame is to nicks and scratches… and we’ve already put a tiny nick in the frame of our review unit, even though all it’s really done is travel from video shoot to video shoot.
  The iPhone 12 Pro’s OLED display is larger than the iPhone 11 Pro, at 6.1 inches, and the phone is slightly taller as a result. The display is otherwise basically the same as last year in terms of brightness and pixel density, which means it looks excellent, although it’s still a 60Hz refresh rate, which at this point is behind virtually every Android phone at $700 and up.
 There are excuses to be made about Apple’s sales volumes and available display panel supply, but in the end, a 60Hz display is simply not very… pro. Indeed, Apple’s own iPad Pro has a ProMotion high refresh rate display. If you’ve only ever had iPhones, you will not really notice this because it is the same as ever. But if you have used a 120Hz display, the difference in smoothness when scrolling is certainly noticeable.
 The bezels and notch at the top of the screen are still the same size, which more or less means eventually you just won’t notice them. Early adopters will notice that some apps need to be updated for this new screen size — Instagram is a little broken, as usual — but the iPhone ecosystem generally adapts quickly, so I’d expect a flurry of app updates to come.
 On the sides of the phone, you’ll find four standard antenna gaps, and US models have a millimeter-wave (mmWave) antenna window for ultrawideband (UWB) 5G on Verizon. Apple told us that holding the phone with your hand over this window shouldn’t affect wireless performance and that there’s no particular guidance on how to hold the phone. I have large hands and basically can’t hold the phone without covering the antenna window unless I try, and I didn’t have any issues during my brief UWB tests.
 The back of the phone features Apple’s new MagSafe magnetic wireless charging and mount system, which feels like the connector equivalent of the summer before college for the first time: the future is bright and exciting, and you get to reinvent your whole situation from scratch. But the days of the Lightning connector are obviously coming to a close, and it’s okay to be sad about it.
 MagSafe is extremely clever as a concept and exceedingly fine in reality. I am excited about magnetic car mounts and stabilizer gimbals and other accessories that don’t require fiddling to attach. I also think there will be neat uses of the additional alignment magnet just below the charging coil; that’s the one that keeps Apple’s just-okay wallet case attached correctly. Apple seems confident that an actual MagSafe accessory ecosystem will develop, unlike the iPad Smart Connector. There are already third-party chargers and car mounts and things being announced. (Apple claims taking its more standard cases on and off the phone is easier because of the magnets, and I have no idea what they’re talking about. It seems just the same as ever.)
  Interesting possibilities aside, the actual MagSafe charging puck is pretty huge for something you might carry around with you even though it’s small for a wireless charger, and I’m not particularly excited about traveling with it in my bag. The cord itself is short, so it does feel like only a baby step toward a version that’ll work while you’re on the couch or in bed with a low battery. Apple has yet to come out with one with a longer cord, but it seems inevitable.
 MagSafe is also expensive. If you want to use Apple’s $39 MagSafe charger so you can get fast 15-watt charging, you’ll need to buy your own 20-watt USB-C power adapter, or shell out for Apple’s at $19 because it’s no longer in the box. (In general, the removal of the power adapter and headphones from the iPhone box this year  seems like a good thing but with mixed environmental repercussions.)
 If you don’t want to pay that much, you can use any regular Qi wireless charger for up to 7.5-watt charging, same as the iPhone 11 line, and Apple says third parties will be able to build approved MagSafe chargers in the future, so hopefully there are some cheaper options soon.
 All in all, MagSafe is a very clever idea that needs some more bake time in the wild before it can actually replace the Lightning connector and deliver the port-less iPhone Apple seems to be headed toward in the future.
   Last year I said the iPhone 11 Pro had the best camera on a smartphone, and it’s not like the iPhone 12 Pro went backward. But it’s only a small step forward — enough to stay just ahead of the competition. Most of the improvements are fairly minor.
 The main camera has a very slightly brighter lens than last year, which helps it in low light, and Apple’s new Smart HDR 3 processing seems to be, well, a little smarter. The noise reduction is improved and looks better than the iPhone 11: photos look less grainy, and there’s a little more detail. The photos are also slightly more contrasty; every year, Apple seems to be more willing to let highlights be highlights and shadows be shadows, which is a look I’m personally fond of.
  All four cameras on the phone can do night mode now, which is very nice to have, but it’s most useful on the front camera for night mode selfies. Using the tele or the ultrawide for night mode shots works, but I’d stick with the main camera. It’s the best camera on the phone, and it takes the best images.
 There are some other small but noticeable photo improvements over the 11 Pro: the ultrawide lens distorts a little less at the edges, and photos from the ultrawide and telephoto are a tad sharper and more detailed. There are also some new settings: you can actually turn lens correction off, and you can turn off both Smart HDR and “scene detection,” which tries to intelligently recognize a shot and expose it correctly. It’s nice to see Apple embrace additional camera options, but I would generally leave everything flipped on; if you are offended by auto camera modes, you should just use an app with good manual controls like Halide.
  The 12 Pro also has a new LIDAR sensor on the back, something we first saw on the iPad Pro. If you’re deep into AR gimmicks, you’re going to love the LIDAR sensor. It makes AR gimmicks better than ever. (I do not love AR gimmicks, but the ones I tried were quite nice.)
 In regular, non-augmented reality, most people will take advantage of the LIDAR sensor on the 12 Pro in low-light situations, where it enables the camera to focus much more quickly — up to six times faster, according to Apple — and also take night mode portrait shots.
  In testing, the focus was indeed fast, and iPhone 12 Pro low-light photos were sharper than the iPhone 11 Pro because it has a brighter lens that allows for faster shutter speeds.
 Night mode portrait shots are remarkably impressive — although they only work with the wide lens, not the tele. Google’s Pixel 5 can take Night Sight portrait photos as well, but it can be fiddly to make it happen. The iPhone 12 Pro just took impressive photos, no fiddling required.
 Unless you are extremely committed to either AR gimmicks or night mode portrait photos, I don’t think you’ll get much value out of the iPhone 12 LIDAR sensor. When you take photos in regular light, the camera focuses just like always; the LIDAR sensor isn’t active. In many ways, it feels like LIDAR is mostly on the phone so that Apple and other people can figure out what to do with it in the future.
 Compared to the Google Pixel 5 and the Samsung Note 20 Ultra, the iPhone 12 Pro delivers exactly what we’ve come to expect from Apple: great photos in almost every case, with balanced colors and great details. I still prefer the Pixel look ever so slightly, and I am reliably informed that there are people who prefer Samsung’s hyperrealistic colors, but the iPhone 11 Pro was the phone to beat. The iPhone 12 Pro slightly improves on the 11 Pro in most situations and adds the ability to get usable shots in even harder edge cases.
  The big thing hanging over all of this is that we have yet to test the iPhone 12 Pro Max, which has a much larger main camera sensor with a new stabilization system. The tele lens on the 12 Pro is nice to have, but there’s a reason Apple puts the ultrawide on the regular 12: it’s just more versatile. If you’re spending extra money because you want a slightly better camera on your iPhone, I’d wait to see if spending just a little bit more extra money can get you a far better camera. This, as my colleague Dan Seifert says, is how they get you.
  Speaking of video, the other big new camera feature across the iPhone 12 line is the ability to create Dolby Vision HDR video. There is no way to talk about this without falling deeply into the weeds of video formats, but I promise you it’s going to be fun. I promise.
 HDR stands for high dynamic range. You’re probably familiar with the term from still photography, where HDR systems in smartphone cameras have been a revolution. But to understand HDR in video, you have to set aside everything you know from photography HDR. It’s very, very different.
 HDR in video basically means that the brightest part of the image can get much brighter, and you can see more detail in the bright parts of an image compared to SDR, or standard dynamic range. This is actually really easy to show on the iPhone. When you shoot video, it captures in HDR, and when you swipe through your camera roll to play it back, you can see the display change modes and get brighter. That’s HDR.
 HDR videos also generally have more available colors in them. SDR videos can have 16 million colors, while HDR videos on the iPhone have 700 million colors. That’s a lot of additional colors, and it means things like gradients and sunsets and so on look smoother. Add it all up, and HDR video is brighter and more colorful than SDR video, which is great.
 Watching HDR video has gotten very easy over the past few years. Almost every new higher-end TV and phone supports HDR, and most of the major streaming services offer movies and TV shows in HDR. (HBO Max is the most notable exception.) But it’s still been hard to  shoot and  share HDR video yourself. It’s not impossible: Samsung phones since the Galaxy S10 can shoot in a format called HDR10+, but it’s a setting that’s tucked deeply away. And if you’re really motivated, there are workflows to get HDR video out of everything from a Sony RX100 Mark V on up. But none of that is easy.
 So the iPhone shooting HDR video by default, out of the box, is a big deal.
  I’ve been saying “HDR” this whole time, not “Dolby Vision.” That’s because Dolby Vision is one kind of HDR — a format — and there are lots of HDR formats. With lots of formats come compatibility problems. And, yep, Dolby Vision on the iPhone 12 has some compatibility problems — but not as many problems as I feared. Apple and Dolby have done something very smart to ensure long-term compatibility at the cost of some short-term incompatibility.
 Dolby Vision and the rival HDR10 format are what’s called  perceptual quantizer (PQ) HDR systems. And PQ systems are not backwards compatible with SDR displays, meaning Dolby Vision video has only been compatible with Dolby Vision displays. (Playing a Dolby Vision video on a non-Dolby Vision display would look completely wrong.) If you wanted to watch a Dolby Vision video on a regular display, you would have to re-encode it into SDR — basically, make an entirely new video file.
 But the iPhone 12 shoots video in a newer version of Dolby Vision called Profile 8.4 that’s built on a standard called HLG, or hybrid log-gamma. HLG works differently than PQ systems; it was developed by broadcasters like the BBC and NHK to be backwards compatible with SDR displays so they would only have to broadcast one video stream.
 All this means that an iPhone 12 HDR video is a 10-bit HLG file with additional Dolby Vision metadata on top, and it will happily play back as SDR on SDR displays, HLG on HLG displays, and Dolby Vision on Dolby Vision displays that support Profile 8.4. And iOS 14 is smart enough to know when the apps and devices you’re sharing video to don’t support this new format, and it’ll make sure you send something that works. It is all very clever, even though, in practice, what it means is you’re mostly sharing SDR video.
  There’s a catch. (Of course.) Dolby Vision Profile 8.4 is a  new version of Dolby Vision, and that means a bunch of older Dolby Vision gear out there needs to be updated to support it. So I can’t promise you any random Dolby Vision TV with AirPlay 2 will play back iPhone 12 Pro video in Dolby Vision. You’ll have to check to see if your TV supports Profile 8.4. Dieter’s older Vizio P65 has AirPlay 2 but played back iPhone 12 Dolby Vision video in SDR. My 2016 LG B6 OLED supports Dolby Vision but not profile 8.4, and it wouldn’t play an iPhone 12 video file from a USB stick at all. It’s also old enough now that it’ll probably never get another software update to do so. And so on.
 That said, new TVs will naturally support the newest versions of Dolby Vision, and you can always just plug in an Apple TV 4K and AirPlay to that. (The way iPhone 12 Dolby Vision video works with an Apple TV is wonky, though: you have to run the Apple TV in Dolby Vision mode all the time to make it work. If you are a sane person and use your Apple TV in SDR with “match content” turned on, you will still always see SDR video from an iPhone 12. I hope Apple fixes this very quickly.)
 Oh, and if you have a Mac, Dolby Vision video won’t be supported until macOS Big Sur comes out, and we don’t really know when that’s going to be. I also don’t know when the likes of Instagram and TikTok and YouTube will support Dolby Vision video from the iPhone; right now, you will see your videos in SDR when shared to those platforms. (YouTube supports HDR video right now, but only in HLG and HDR10. Formats!)
 So Dolby Vision video on the iPhone is a little complicated, and there will be some compatibility goofiness at the start. But over time, I’d expect it to fade away. TV manufacturers and social platforms have a lot of incentives to figure out how to play back people’s iPhone videos well, after all.
 And in the meantime, it’s true that most people watch their smartphone videos  on their smartphones, and iPhone 12 Dolby Vision video looks very bright and colorful on the iPhone 12 Pro and other newer iPhones and iPads that support HDR. I’m excited it’s there and am even more excited it is the default.
 I was lucky enough to work on this review with  Verge video director Becca Farsace, so I asked her for some thoughts on iPhone 12 Pro video quality in general. Here’s what she had to say:
 There are three things I noticed when shooting video with the iPhone 12 Pro: a great level of detail, brighter footage at night, and light reflections for days. The iPhone 12 Pro can shoot up to 4K at 60fps and 1080p at 240fps, just like the 11 Pro. I was impressed with the video capabilities of the iPhone 11 Pro last year, and with the iPhone 12 Pro, it’s no different… like, it’s actually hard to tell the difference between footage from the 11 and the 12.
 In perfect lighting conditions, the video is beautiful. Colors are punchy, details are crisp, and it can handle skin tones well. The 12’s footage is the slightest bit warmer during the day, but unless you are really trying to find a difference between these cameras, you won’t.
 At night, the 12 Pro is a bit brighter overall and does a nice job of smoothing the dark areas to combat noise. The main sensor is certainly the brightest, and once you switch over to the ultrawide, things get a bit dark. But more concerning is when shooting at night, and using that main sensor, there are still little dots all over the frame! Any light coming directly into the lens, such as headlights from cars, casts lots of light reflections onto the image, and it’s quite distracting. This was a problem with footag

title: IPhone 12 Pro评测：拥有出色的摄像头和性能，Dolby Vision视频非常棒，5G可以非常快，但续航时间不如iPhone 11 Pro
title_en: iPhone 12 Pro review: has an excellent camera and performance, Dolby Vision video is great, 5G can be very fast, but battery life is inferior to iPhone 11 Pro

与大多数年份相比，iPhone12有很多新功能。苹果已经安装了通常的升级，比如更快的处理器和改进的摄像头，但这是我们意料之中的事情。除此之外，还有新的设计，新的OLED屏幕，全新的充电和配件生态系统，以及MagSafe，当然还有5G。
看起来很明显，苹果和它的运营商合作伙伴都在努力结盟，以使这一升级成为一个超级周期。所有这些新产品还伴随着更高的价格(基本64 GB机型为829美元)、折扣以及大量营销的运营商折价和分期付款计划。在大流行引发的经济低迷中，这可能是一个艰难的推销。
在你需要新手机的时候推荐默认的iPhone很容易，但要说所有这些新东西加起来是否会迫使你比计划的更早升级就难得多了。在你需要新手机的时候，推荐默认的iPhone很容易，但很难说所有这些新东西加起来是否会迫使你比计划的更早升级。
尼莱·帕特尔(Nilay Patel)正在单独评估iPhone12Pro，请看这里。较小的iPhone12 Mini和配备更好摄像头的iPhone12Pro Max要过一段时间才会上市。有理由考虑所有这些，但我认为大多数需要新iPhone的人都会想要标准的iPhone12。
我喜欢iPhone 12的新设计。除了比iPhone 11更小更轻之外，它还有扁平的侧面和平板屏幕。从2014年的iPhone6开始，我们在iPhone上的弧形边缘已经生活了六年。所以我的部分感觉可能就是iPhone12给人的感觉是新的。但它也给人一种回到iPhone4和iPhone5的感觉，这是我真正喜欢的最后两款iPhone。
尽管所有这些平坦的边缘，接缝和角落都有足够的斜面，让它拿起来很舒服。IPhone12的导轨是哑光铝制的，与Pro型号的光滑钢材相比，我更喜欢它们。不幸的是，后部玻璃超级有光泽，极易发现指纹，而且一如既往地容易发现微小的微小磨损。不管怎样，大多数人都会把箱子放在手机上。
从技术上讲，iPhone12的正面根本不是玻璃，而是一种玻璃和陶瓷的混合体，苹果公司将其命名为“陶瓷盾牌”。它在玻璃内部使用陶瓷晶体来提高iPhone11的抗跌落能力。苹果公司表示，它比iPhone11好四倍，这是一件好事，因为今年的屏幕维修成本有所上升。我不能用我们的审查装置来测试这一点(至少，不是故意的)，但我确实从3英尺高的地方完全意外地掉到了混凝土上，这让我的心脏停止了跳动，但只在铝板上留下了一个几乎看不见的响声。耐刮性应该和去年差不多。
如果你看铝制导轨，你会看到一串天线线，甚至在边上有一个用于超宽带(UWB)毫米波天线的小塑料部分。它们稍微打破了对称性(尤其是底部)，但我并不介意。我在这一点上也变成了切口盲，但我应该注意到，Face ID仍然需要在屏幕顶部有一个非常大的裁剪。
那里没有指纹传感器，虽然我真的没想到会有，但在大流行期间，这是一件令人沮丧的事情。我想，对于Android制造商来说，最大的一线希望是，当你戴着面具时，他们的手机更容易进入。
但总体而言，这种设计感觉比过去几年的iPhone更优雅、更自信，甚至比iPhone X的大重新设计也不例外。更小的尺寸是最好的部分；iPhoneXR和iPhone11总是感觉有点太大了。如果这就是我们未来六年的设计，我不会抱怨。
苹果能够缩小iPhone12尺寸的原因之一是它改用了OLED屏幕。这有助于减少边框，甚至在手机周围也能保持完美，同时保持实际可见屏幕与iPhone 11相同的6.1英寸。与LCD相比，我更喜欢OLED的外观，因为它的黑色更深，所以我一直在等待这个变化。
在改用OLED的同时，苹果也将像素数增加到了1080x2340。如果你没有沉浸在智能手机技术的讨论中，你很幸运地避免了抱怨之前的iPhone在技术上甚至没有达到1080p。现在他们做到了，这些抱怨终于可以停止了。
OLED屏幕非常棒，对比度和颜色都很好，但刷新率不高。
但它们将被另一个争论所取代：iPhone是否应该有一个高刷新率的屏幕。许多这个价位的Android手机(几乎所有价格更高的手机)都有90或120 Hz的刷新率，这使得滚动和动画更流畅。IPhone的锁定频率和往常一样是60赫兹。
这是一个技术规格的争论，但当你在手机上滚动或导航时，你可以感觉到这一点。苹果在iPad Pro上配备了这样的屏幕。我认为iPhone12没有使用它有两个原因：第一，iOS本身就已经感觉流畅和快速，第二，这是价格较低的iPhone，所以看到标准的刷新率并不令人惊讶。缺少120赫兹的iPhone12Pro机型更加刺耳。
与这款手机相比，iPhone12Pro确实有一个屏幕优势：在正常使用时，它可以达到更高的亮度水平。然而，我对这款iPhone12的屏幕亮度没有任何抱怨，即使是在户外。而在观看HDR内容时，两款手机仍可达到1200尼特的峰值亮度。
总体而言，OLED屏幕对我来说是一个明显的改进，主要是因为它的对比度和HDR都有所改善，而不是像素数量。我还很高兴地报告，苹果在色彩科学上也没有妥协--颜色一如既往地准确。
在iPhone12的所有新功能中，我认为MagSafe是最有趣的。这是一个完整的系统，可以通过磁铁将东西连接到iPhone的背面：无线充电器、保护套、车座、钱包，最终还有其他东西，比如万向架或我还没有想过的东西。
首屈一指的配件是MagSafe充电器，这是一款售价39美元的冰球，它可以发出令人满意的咔哒声，贴在手机背面，然后无线充电。IPhone有第二个用于MagSafe的NFC芯片，可以识别连接到它上的东西。当它是MagSafe充电器时，只要你有至少20瓦的电源块，它将允许手机以15瓦的功率供电，而不是通常的7.5瓦。(MagSafe充电器没有附带充电器。苹果将以19美元的价格向你出售一个20瓦的USB-C充电器，或者你也可以使用笔记本电脑等其他设备上的充电器。)。
这就是无线充电速度慢得令人恼火和可以接受之间的区别。在我的测试中，我会在一个小时内充电40%左右。这比目前最快的无线充电系统慢得多，也比有线慢得多，但它也很容易和方便。
苹果的冰球又薄又轻，附着牢固，你可以拿起手机使用，而不会断开连接。如果其他人想要制造充电冰球，他们需要使用苹果的“为iPhone制造”(MFI)程序。其他快速无线充电器仍然以通常的费率为iPhone充电；新的充电器将需要使用NFC握手，以实现更快的充电速度。(不，应用程序不能直接访问这个NFC芯片，所以支付和其他用途仍然比Android更严格。)。
NFC还可以让苹果做一些可爱的小事情，比如当它检测到附件已经连接时，就会点亮屏幕上的一个戒指。例如，蓝色的箱子有一个蓝色的戒指。苹果公司正在销售一系列MagSafe表壳，它声称这些表壳更容易打开和关闭，但它们与前几年的硅胶表壳没有明显的不同。还有一个钱包，可以容纳三张带有特殊屏蔽的卡，以防止它们被消磁。就它的样子来说，它已经足够好了，但是你必须把它取下来才能拿出一张卡，或者给手机无线充电。
我很高兴看到第三方公司能为这些磁铁拿出什么产品。他们不需要参加MFI就可以简单地制造磁性配件，而且汽车悬置已经在路上了。我也希望能看到一些摄影和录像配件。磁铁足够强大，可以支撑手机抵御光线的碰撞，但像自行车支架这样的东西仍然需要更坚固的附件。
在我们谈到这个话题的同时，最后再说一句关于收费的问题。苹果确实已经把交流适配器从盒子里拿出来了，还附带了一根USB-C转闪电电缆，所以如果你没有USB-C充电器，你就需要买一个。尽管环境效益可能不大，但我仍然支持这一决定。
我不太赞成坚持在闪电端口充电的决定。一次重大的重新设计是一个切换到更常见的USB-C端口的机会，苹果自己的电脑和平板电脑与其他所有Android手机和许多其他设备都使用这个端口。苹果没有勇气这么做的事实告诉我，它的长期计划可能更多地与MagSafe有关，而不是其他任何事情。我不喜欢Lightning，但我必须承认，当谈到有线充电时，总比什么都没有要好。
苹果已经加入了5G炒作产业综合体。该公司正在推广5G，将其作为其所有新iPhone机型的旗舰功能，并承诺以令人难以置信的快速下载速度。在美国的现实情况是，获得这样的速度是在正确的城市，有时是正确的城市街区的问题。见鬼，5G的超高速毫米波(MmWave)版本需要在正确的街角。
这些网络还没有建成，尽管运营商做出了崇高的承诺，但我不知道要多久才能建成。
您的5G体验将因您所在的位置、运营商和数据计划而大不相同。
在我在奥克兰和旧金山对T-Mobile和Verizon进行的测试中，我发现自己使用LTE信号的频率与5G一样频繁-如果不是更频繁的话。当我真的得到5G的时候，我的下载速度从略高于LTE到大约快两倍不等。但当我有意识地寻找Verizon的mmWave网络所在的人行道时，那里的速度令人难以置信。
所有这一切都是说，你的5G体验完全取决于你在哪里，你在什么网络上，以及你有多幸运地找到了正确的信号。获得2400 Mbps的速度并用它下载整个Netflix赛季真的是令人敬畏。走半个街区，看到车速降到普通的老式LTE速度，这可不是什么大不了的事。
就iPhone本身而言，它的表现与5G Android手机不相上下，甚至更好。它当然可以处理速度，而且它似乎可以毫无问题地接收到信号。它在mmWave上确实会变热，如果你过度使用，它会显著影响电池寿命。然而，苹果在iOS的幕后做了大量工作，以调整5G体验。
默认情况下，每部5G手机都会启用“智能数据模式”。它会将iPhone12的速度降到LTE，除非苹果的软件认为你真的需要它们。目前还不清楚什么情况会触发5G速度的提升，但这是你下载的数据类型以及你使用的应用程序的某种组合。
奇怪的是，当iPhone将你的速度限制在LTE时，它仍然会在状态栏中显示5G图标。它现在是您可用的最佳速度的指示器，而不是正在使用的实际连接类型。如果你愿意，你可以关闭智能数据，但我让它开着，老实说，我从来没有真正感觉到我被扼杀了。
(只要我们讨论的是不准确的状态栏图标，我就会指出AT&amp；T为其LTE Advanced网络设计的欺骗性“5G E”指示器仍然存在。美国电话电报公司(AT&&T)和苹果公司(Apple)都为这种品牌推广感到羞耻。)。
5G还有其他细微差别。尽管苹果在确保iPhone12可以在任何美国网络上运行方面做得非常出色，但在国际上还是存在一些频段差异(不一定包括mmWave)，所以如果你搬家了，或者是否还能再次旅行，请仔细检查一下。如果你在iPhone上同时使用物理SIM卡和eSIM卡，事实证明双SIM卡双待机规格无法处理同时在两个网络上亮起5G的情况。如果您将它们都激活，您将获得4G。但你可以单独获得5G。
包括Verizon在内的一些网络要求你购买一张新的5G兼容SIM卡。因此，如果你没有获得5G，而你认为你应该获得5G，这可能就是问题所在。您可能还需要调整您的计划。
IPhone也会尽量了解你的数据套餐，如果它知道你的数据套餐是无限的，它会更自由地使用5G来做某些事情。苹果甚至会允许它通过5G下载完整的iOS更新，如果你是无限制的话。如果你改变了你的计划，或者不想让它这么做，你可能需要通过各种设置去潜水。
苹果还表示，多亏了它所做的优化，通过Wi-Fi进行的捆绑速度可能会比通过有线闪电电缆捆绑的速度更快。在最佳条件下(可能包括系留设备上的Wi-Fi 6)，Wi-Fi系留可能比以前快四倍。因为找到mmWave信号对我来说都很麻烦，所以我还没有机会对其进行全面测试。
最后，如果你的运营商不直接与苹果合作来点亮你的iPhone，5G就不会在你的iPhone上工作。与以前的网络不同，你不能进去，只需手动设置APN和彩信设置，就可以很好地进行5G了。不过，对于绝大多数人来说，这应该不是问题。苹果已经与30个市场的100多家运营商合作，在iPhone上启用5G，包括美国所有三大运营商-但如果你使用MVNO，你应该在购买之前仔细检查5G是否有效。
你真的不应该担心这些5G的大部分细节。如果你在生活和工作的地方碰巧获得了5G速度，那就为你欺负吧。如果你不这样做，他们很快就会来找你，希望网络会继续快速，即使在所有这些5G iPhone开始填满频道之后。无论哪种方式，现在仅仅因为iPhone有5G而购买iPhone都不是一个好主意。这是一笔不错的奖金，但还不会更多。
IPhone12中摄像头最重要的变化不在传感器或镜头上。它们完全没有变化，除了主广角相机从ƒ/1.8光圈调到ƒ/1.6光圈，以允许更多的光线进入。相反，更大的区别来自软件和解锁新功能，这要归功于新的A14仿生处理器，它可以在手机上运行一切。
IPhone12摄像头比iPhone11有了明显的改进，虽然不是很大。
如果你在比较iPhone12的型号，关键要知道的是，普通的iPhone12和12 Mini有相同的摄像头系统。IPhone12Pro增加了长焦，用于微光聚焦和深度计算的LIDAR，以及一些更多的照片和视频编码选项。IPhone12 Pro Max有了更大的升级，配备了更大的主摄像头传感器和更高的图像稳定度。
尼莱·帕特尔(Nilay Patel)对iPhone12Pro的评论详细介绍了它的一些独特功能，并对视频质量进行了更深入的探讨。苹果公司在HDR方面大肆宣扬，称这款相机为杜比视觉相机(Dolby Vision Camera)。我会让尼莱告诉你所有的细节。就我而言，我只想说，视频质量仍然很好，只有Galaxy Note 20 Ultra能挑战它，但我认为iPhone更有优势。
但回到A14仿生。它为iPhone12上的所有摄像头(包括前置自拍摄像头)解锁了夜间模式，甚至还解锁了夜间人像模式。苹果公司还对其系统进行了一些调整，将多帧图像组合成一张图像，名为Smart HDR 3。该系统还应该能更好地识别人脸或天空等事物，并调整照片以优化其外观。事实上，如果你想切换它，甚至还有一个新的设置叫做“场景检测”。
一般来说，在常规照明条件下，iPhone12在细节方面做得更好。苹果公司表示，这可能是因为它今年在更多的情况下应用了深度融合算法。我仍然喜欢Pixel的签名，对比y的外观，但苹果似乎只是朝着它的方向前进了一点。IPhone12的照片似乎终于从脸部过度明亮的阴影中后退了一步。但这是一个很小的调整，更大的变化是在更极端的条件下发生的。
与iPhone11相比，iPhone12需要进入夜间模式的次数较少，这要归功于速度更快的镜头。即使它发生了，我也能拍得更清晰、更明亮。在最黑暗的条件下，Pixel5仍然能更好地操控自己，但它比以往任何时候都要近得多。
夜间模式肖像是主要的新功能之一，它们值得一试，但它们看起来不错的照明条件范围并不是很大。一件好事是，iPhone12以出色的颜色通过了眼镜测试；它们似乎永远不会意外变得模糊。我对夜间模式的自拍印象较差。在这种模式下无法关闭屏幕闪光灯，如果你戴眼镜，这是一个问题。
超宽幅相机也进行了许多软件增强，因此它的质量也得到了改善。然而，它仍然是一个从根本上更糟糕的传感器和镜头组合，如果你仔细观察，你可以看到细节中的颗粒。不过，这对风景是有好处的。
苹果公司表示，它已经做了更多的调整，以补偿超宽镜头边缘的镜头失真，特别是在脸部和建筑方面。我确实认为这有帮助，但只有一点点。这张金门大桥的照片仍然有一个明显的转弯。
对我来说，相机的底线是我肯定看到了比iPhone11有明显的改进，但它们不足以迫使升级。这并不意味着iPhone12不是一款非常好的相机。是的，性能、简单性和纯粹的良好质量的结合继续给人留下深刻的印象。
IPhone12的速度如此之快，你应该不会感到惊讶。新的A14仿生芯片建立在新的5纳米工艺之上-我们预计这也将为即将到来的Mac电脑提供动力。游戏加载速度快，没有延迟，网页渲染没有问题，我发现在多任务处理时需要重新加载的应用程序更少了。
电池续航时间是可以接受的，但没有完全达到iPhone11的水平-而且要注意你的UWB 5G使用情况。
但在实际的日常使用中，我怀疑没有人会看到iPhone11与iPhone11有明显的不同，iPhone11过去是，现在也是非常快的。IPhone12速度的重要性不在于它现在很快，而在于它可能在三年甚至五年后仍然很快。
电池续航时间不错，但与iPhone11(绝对的电池冠军)相比，这似乎是一个小小的倒退。苹果觉得有必要创造一种静默关闭5G的特殊模式，在续航方面有点令人担忧。幸运的是，我认为iPhone12的电池续航能力一点也不差。我可以安然无恙地度过一整天。另一方面，我必须承认，比起iPhone11，用一整天的大量使用来扼杀这款手机要容易得多。
至于iOS14，我会告诉你克里斯·韦尔奇(Chris Welch)的评论，因为他的想法反映了我的想法。我真的很喜欢主屏幕上有小工具，虽然我希望从我最喜欢的应用程序中有更多的选择。说到愿望，我希望苹果能在制造Siri上下更大的功夫。
.
